none.justification: Correctly flags that src/utils/list.c does not exist, gives the exact struct definition, and redirects to the header-only implementation. It lists all six static inline functions and the relevant macros, matching the GT.

ccodegraph.justification: Fully correct: explicitly identifies the false premise about list.c, provides the struct definition, and enumerates all six inline functions plus the macro API in list.h. The extra note about ccodegraph/call graph behavior is not harmful.

codegraph.justification: Fully matches the GT by stating list.c is absent, giving the correct struct fields and line range, and listing all six static inline functions plus the iteration/access macros. No fabrication or material omission.

cbm.justification: Correctly reports the nonexistent list.c and header-only implementation, then lists the complete set of six functions and the relevant macros. The opening phrasing is slightly self-referential but does not affect correctness.
